message:
test(readme): SqlColumnSchema declared_type/autoincrement/default_value/comment

SqlColumnSchema has 7 fields. Existing SQL tests covered name,
nullable, and primary_key_ordinal. The other 4 fields were
uncovered:

- declared_type (Option<String>): backend's declared SQL type
- default_value (Option<String>): DEFAULT clause text
- comment (Option<String>): column-level comment
- autoincrement (bool): SQLAlchemy.Inspector get_columns() parity
  field added in fd90.37. SQLite detection rule: INTEGER + sole
  PK = autoincrement. A regression in the detection logic would
  not have surfaced.

Extends readme_quick_start_round_trip_through_sqlite with assertions
on parent.id (INTEGER PRIMARY KEY) and parent.name (TEXT NOT NULL):

parent.id:
- declared_type ≈ "INTEGER" (case-insensitive match)
- autoincrement = true (SQLite INTEGER PRIMARY KEY rowid alias)
- default_value = None
- comment = None (SQLite has no column-comment storage)

parent.name:
- declared_type ≈ "TEXT"
- autoincrement = false (TEXT is never auto-increment)

Closes frankenpandas-fd90.53.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
